_comment: Runner registry for goal-bridge.
Each key is a runner name. The bridge selects the entry matching its <runner> argument.
format: 'ndjson' | 'line' (default 'line')
ndjson — runner emits NDJSON events on stdout (spec §18 Q1 answer: Codex --json mode).
Bridge uses spawn-per-turn model; reads thread.started / turn.completed /
turn.failed / error events. Relay logic activates on turn.failed.
line   — legacy mode: tail runner stderr for rate_limit / server_error regex patterns.
Claude Code stays here — CC continuation is handled by the Stop hook, not
by the bridge's spawn loop.
spawn: command + args used to start the runner for the FIRST turn.
Substitution tokens expanded at spawn time: {root}
For ndjson runners, the objective is piped on stdin.
For line runners, spawn starts a long-lived child process.
spawn_resume: command + args for SUBSEQUENT turns (ndjson runners only).
Substitution tokens: {root}, {session_id}
The continuation prompt is piped on stdin.
rate_limit / server_error: arrays of regex patterns.
line mode: matched against runner stderr line-by-line.
ndjson mode: matched against the event payload string in turn.failed / error events.
Each string is compiled as a case-insensitive JavaScript RegExp.
provider: maps the runner to a provider key in .goal/quota.json.
claude-code → anthropic; codex → openai.
Env override (any runner): GOAL_BRIDGE_SPAWN_<RUNNER_UPPER>=<space-separated cmd>
e.g. GOAL_BRIDGE_SPAWN_CODEX='codex --api-key sk-...'
